text: Honestly the service is pretty good but the website is a nightmare and the equipment plans are awful (buy phones from anyone else).  Am seriously considering switching because of how bad website is.

Started with T Mobile in 2003 and was a happy customer until today. 22 months ago I signed up for a deal to add another line to my account for $10 a month. My current deal at that time was 3 lines for $100.  I asked the representative at least twice if I could go back to my original plan if I wanted to and she said at any time I chose.  At that time I had 4 children aged 7 to 2 so life was busy and I did not have time to scrutinize every bill, but my 1st bill was $176 so I figured $110 plus the 60 in equipment charges and tax was about right. Turns out that I was getting overcharged by a lot for almost 2 years and when I finally spoke to a supervisor today he said he couldn't give me the old deal and that there was nothing he could do.  Wow so much for being a loyal customer and never being late with payments for 16 years. Looking for another carrier extremely displeased to say the least.

Will never use T-mobile again. They not only lied to me in the store but repeatedly told me that I did not have a balance due on my account but then would call me repeatedly saying that there was a balance. Horrible company, horrible experience. And was lied to on a regular basis. Never again

TMobile has stolen money from me I made a payment of 106.61 for one months bill it was never posted I have the receipt where I payed it and now they refuse to post the payment I have talked to managers at the location who tell me to call customer service who then tell me to contact the store I get pushed back and forth and I am sick of it they claim there was a refund but there was never a refund and they refuse to produce anything showing where this supposed refund took place I am regretting ever leaving Verizon my bill is higher and my money has been stolen

worst company to deal with. My son is a brittle diabetic and need a good phone for his medical safety, he has turned his phone for not working nine time this year so far, they will not give him a phone that he knows work for him which is a down grade phone. they do not care if he dies. he has a service dog to alert him if he goes low and is close to going into a coma which may kill him. they told me it is against policy to just give him a phone that works. he has to pay $195 so his phone is 1/2 paid off then they may let him down grade to another phone my sons life means nothing to them, I hope someone in the company needs help and are refused help and they die. THIS COMPANY SUCKS BIG TIME IF YOU USE THEM BEWARE THEY ARE NOT YOUR FRIENDLY COMPANY NO NOT USE DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY. iF MY SON DIES BECAUSE OF THIS COMPANY i WILL SUE THEM  FOR  DO NOT DO NOT USE T MOBIL

A representative came in our store and pushed us into signing up even though my husband kept telling them we weren’t interested. Next thing we had phones at our door and they told us if we didn’t call our previous service to get a pin # our current phones would be disconnected. Never heard of such a thing! He took my phone and looked up info, which was odd too. We told them we didn’t want to sign up with them to cancel everything. They said they did, but we have gotten 3 bills since and every time I call they say we have a $0 balance. Well, today I received a collection notice from a collection agency!! T- Mobile is a FRAUD! DO NOT USE THEM!!

I had no issues with T-Mobile until the end when we were trying to cancel our account because we were planning on moving. First of all, I had to pay over $100 just to cancel (this was not including paying off my phone or Apple Watch, I had already done that). Second, after we cancelled for about a month we got an auto withdrawal for the same monthly amount as usual. When we called they said that since we canceled on the same day as the new cycle began, we had to pay for the whole last month instead of just the day use. If that’s a policy then fine but at least tell me that when I cancel! The amount was also including payments for items that I previously paid off to cancel! I told them it was a mistake but they said there was nothing they could do after talking to 2 different managers. Look, if that’s some sort of policy that’s fine but why didn’t they tell me that as a heads up when I talked to them in the first place? We though that was the end of it but today, 3 months later we got another auto withdrawal from T-Mobile!! We called customer service AGAIN & they said that apparently the tablet we got for free (as a promotion a while ago) wasn’t paid off & needed to be paid off!! So why did they tell me I paid everything off months before & why are they making me finish paying for it if it was free over a year ago??? Overall, just to cancel we ended up paying over $500 AFTER paying off our devices. This company cares about you until you want to cancel & then they don’t care. Their service is horrible & the company we are with now, we are paying less for 2 phones than we were with T-Mobile for 1 phone. Will never go back.
T-MOBILE IS THE BEST! BEST PRICES, BEST OVERALL SERVICES, ETC., HANDS DOWN!
Tmobile is back to being the worst company ! They get good and than worse again it’s amAzing ! Absolutely the worst

I’ve been with T-Mobile for four years I haven’t got an upgrade but when I went for an upgrade it’s not really an upgrade do you have to pay full price for your phone that you want what is the point of upgrade this company sucks really bad I will stay away from them The Word upgrade they need to take off because they’re screwing people

If I could give zero stars I would. Customer service is garbage to start. Not to mention even with a "high speed" unlimited data plan im still waiting 10 minutes to get a GIF bar to load no matter where im at. Without connection to WiFi my data plan might as well be a grown a** adult sitting at Denny's by himself with a box of crayons and a kids menu. Pi**  poor overall experience. I've been with t mobile for years. I'd rather go back to flip phones and laptops than give these clowns anymore money.

This one is long. I originally placed an order over the phone as a new customer made my payment and everything went smoothly...or so I thought. I was told I would receive a UPS tracking number by email that same night this was 10/31/19, I ordered the iPhone 11 in white which they told me they had in stock so the shipping process SHOULD have started by 11/1/19 with an estimated delivery date of 11/4/19-11/6/19. I kept checking the order status and it kept saying order being processed estimated shipping date unavailable. I called 11/1/19 and was told it should be shipped Monday 11/4/19 checked again to the same results called TMobile again and they said it was backordered even though 2 different people had already told me it was in stock and then I was told it's probably because someone placed the order before I did. Checked the website to find out the same shipping dates are on there and the phone is in "stock", and that I should receive my ups tracking number no later than 10PM Monday night 11/4/19 checked again the next day and it was still being processed! Called again and was told my order was "frozen" and that they would have to cancel the order and that I can pick up the phone in a store nearest to me. This is where I effing lost because I asked the representative since I've already made the payment of I would have to pay again she told me " no payment will transfer over" get to the store only to find out that THEY DO NOT TRANSFER PAYMENTS the rep at the store was extremely helpful and called their online/phone order services. Was told AGAIN that I will receive a UPS tracking number that night 11/5/19 because the order was already being shipped and it was too late to cancel. Still no tracking number!!! Called again today 11/6/19 and find out the cancellation did in fact take effect and that I will not be shipped anything!!! In total I spoke to 6 different representatives only to be told something different by each. No one knew what the f@#$ they were talking about. Now I have to wait a whole week for my refund to be issued so I can just get my phone directly from the store. I advise against doing anything over the phone or online with T-Mobile. They have absolutely no idea what their doing and still blamed me for the confusion. Waste of my time and money. Unfortunately since I have an active account I'm stuck with them. Not only that but my line is currently active and they offered no incentives considering I'll be phoneless for another week. Terrible experience all around. I do not blame TMobile as a whole because in store they explain everything very well and we're actually concerned over what was going on. I'm not saying to not switch to T-Mobile but STAY AWAY FROM ONLINE OR PHONE ORDERS they will just give you a run around.

Me and my friend have been with T Mobile. They charged my friend for 10 lines when he only opened two(him and his wife)...... *****!! They took money from him. You then constantly charged and are charging my friend for things he paid off months ago after leaving and switching. As for me, your insurance is a scam. You sent me three defective phones that I finally canceled and had Samsung fix it and they confirmed you guys were sending broken phones. Your insurance is a scam. You also hung up when I was calling about this and you tell me there is nothing you can do??? You also put a lot of bloatware all over my phone without telling me, you tell me false information about the defective phones, and you guys ignore my frustration and try to get me to buy a new line. F You don't care about customers and you take money from people and you guys are nothing but a scam. No wonder why there are so many negative reviews. You suck T Mobile. Oh and me and my friend will be going to a lawyer and going to sue you for fraud. Bye :)

The worst customer services I have ever experienced. Spent 5 hours with their customer services about switch a family plan. Every time they promised that they would call back in 1 days, but never heard from them after hang out the phone!Also, I had to explained the situations 5 times with different people. 

Also, I spent over 4 hours about my $100 deposit refund issue, their manager promised me to refund and provided us his name and work ID. And then, nothing was refunded within 2 weeks. Never heard from them. 

Is there promise means nothing??? T-mobile sucks.
I'm tired of dealing with T-mobile garbage customer services.

The worst phone company ever. I had ATT, Sprint and T-Mobile. I can't even make phone calls from my own lines in the same account. I called three times, spoke with customer service. The call got dropped all three times after spending about half an hour with each. I waited, hoping they would call back. Nope.

I use love T-Mobile phone services, but now ever since I had financial problems, this company changed on me. The customer services are terrible, owner and people do not care about their customers at all. This company took $106.84 out of my bank account and there was no money in my bank account. My account was not suspended at that time when this happened. I try to work things out with the company but the problem was never resolved and they canceled my account. They also charge you all of the fees and do not work around people's So I decided to pay the remaining balance until I finish paying the balance off and using better phone service that is better than T-Mobile. I hope this company goes out of business. I will never do services with this company again.

T-Mobile claims that it has enhanced it's service. It seems to have advanced only within the city limits. Service in rural areas seems to have been better 7 or 8 years ago when I used a 2g flip phone and was able to roam to an AT&T antenna. Now I can no longer roam in many areas and I have no service even though the new map indicates that I do. I can only assume that  T-Mobile has lost quite a bit of it's roaming capabilities. So much for unlimited roaming.  Anyway, in the city limits, I can deal with it, In rural areas, forget about it.  I just wish that they wouldn't lie about there service.

As my girlfriend and I entered T-Mobile Store 135 (19250 West Lake Houston Parkway, Humble, Texas 77346), Samantha and Eduardo greeted us warmly. During our meeting, Samantha and Eduardo, a formidable and unrivaled team, certainly demonstrated their extensive knowledge of T-Mobile’s advantages compared to other providers and of T-Mobile's products. They answered all our questions in depth including but not limited to coverages, download speeds, Dolby sound, Netflix, transferring data, insurance, and operation of the Samsung Galaxy 10e and 10+ phones.  In fact, they stayed with my girlfriend and me until all our questions had been answered in depth even though it was close to an hour after closing time. Because of their combined expertise, engaging dispositions, and impressive work ethic, we decided to become part of the T-Mobile family.

I've been on the phone with my team of experts for 3 hrs and still don't have any any answers. I've been transferred to person to person and still no answer. I would like to speak with you someone from the Chicago market but it seems like I can't get to anyone from out here.

The price was fair but everything else was a nightmare. I should have read the reviews of others before hooking up with T Mobile. Poor service in all aspects of their business. I could write for hours about how bad our experience was but many others already have. This is the provider for you if you are just looking for price and do not care about anything else. Beware.

T Mobile presents well.Their packages are appealing and cost friendly. However, my experience with the local store in Hazleton Pa. left me "shaking my head" in disbelief at the salesperson's  lack in experience and knowledge of T Mobile policies. Experienced management oversite was non existent. Quaity WiFI and internet speed were emphasized as a top priority due to where we live. We were assured that cell service was 4G in our area. In reality the hot spot was so slow that I could only get sufficient speed late at night, never during the work day.
There is an unbelievable disconnect with dissemination of INCORRECT INFORMATION between  this store and T Mobile's "team of experts." The only good   thing is there is no contract.

Advertised as best coverage, affirmed by salesman.  Not good at all in our area, SW Mo. Also, "bait and switch" on new customer "deals".  MLB should reconsider them as advertising during their games..

We were really impressed with the customer service in the T Mobile store, Atlantic Ave, Brooklyn. Adonis identified and fixed our mobile problem quickly and efficiently. A really nice guy. We can’t thank him enough.

This company isn't even able to provide an easy refill service for your prepaid card. 
- I can't login to my account as it keeps telling me "You are part of a multi-line account and the primary line is already registered" and I should "see the account owner". I AM THE ACCOUNT OWNER!
- The refill link provided in the reminder SMS leads you to a "page not found". Wow, this still happens in 2019?
- After completing the refill form it just don't let you hit "submit". The button is grayed out without giving you any reason
- Keeps telling me my credit card number is invalid. Well, used it last time with T-Mobile why should it be invalid now? It's just a regular debit card, nothing fancy you would think. 
- Transactions fail in 4 out of 5 times. That's so 90s.

I usually spend half an hour for a recurring process which should be as simple as counting to 3. 

SHAME ON YOU T-MOBILE. SEE YOU AT VERIZON.

Only 1 star for great price plans. Otherwise I'd give them 0 for service. Great price plans mean nothing when the power goes out for days and I loose complete service.  T-Mobile was the only provider who lost service.  was pi$$ed sitting next to the Cricket customer who was live streaming an NFL game. .. CRICKET!!  Then, I also wasn't able to receive texts or call alerts that my children's school was in lockdown  for safety reasons not connected to the power outage!  When my elderly parents were struggling with their generator in the middle of the night, they were unable to call me.  When I called TMobile from my mother's AT&T phone, they wanted to send me a text verification code even after I told them I wasn't able to receive text messages! He said the company was able to ensure emergency calls would go out. I clarified I wasn't able to call 911 or 611- thus why I was calling from my mother's AT&T phone!  Making matters worse, news investigations into the issue determined TMobile chooses to not have a backup system.  Low price plans are great but not worth it if I cant communicate with the people who need me most in an emergency.

Disgusting company with incredibly rude customer service and Supervisors. When canceled my service and switched to another carrier they billed me for 2 more month, when i called customer service and explained them situation, representative apologized and said it will be fixed just wait till the correct billed will be mailed to you. I waited and 2 weeks later i receive a letter from collection agency with the same wrong amount. Killed my credit. When called customer service again they had no idea that i called 2 weeks ago and nothing was changed, i asked for a supervisor, they transferred me, explained the situation, i was really upset, what did supervisor said? -Hold on the line sir i will check everything, and HUNG UP!

I was with them for 14 years, and they got worse over the last few. Poor customer service,  and spotty coverage. I canceled my plan, and they have tried to charge me for the last two months that I haven't even been a customer.

Worst customer service ever. I am all about giving an opportunity to correct issues. Hey life happens right? But when you have to call repeatedly to get them to do what they said they would do it is to much. If i could do it again I would chose a different carrier.

Needed mobile coverage on my European phone for an extended stay in Rio Rancho, NM. For my Samsung phone the two options were T-mobile and AT&T. TheT-mobile coverage map showed good coverage where I was  staying. Service personnel swapped my phone chip and were courteous and helpful, but coverage at my location was poor, internet slow or unavailable, and phone service with cut outs and occasionally dropping the line. At times, it was necessary to go outside and orient my phone in a certain direction to get internet coverage, and sending photos was difficult and time-consuming. Will be trying AT&T on my next visit.

The absolute worst service. My husband is oversees and they gave him the wrong SIM card, which won’t help him b/c he doesn’t have WiFi except his room! He needs his phone for many reasons, being a senior officer. I am so fed up with their service. Not only have they not fixed the problem but they won’t refund us. I 150% discourage anyone from using T mobile.

I bought a used iPhone Xs and upon trying to get service on it, was told that there was a lock on it, due to non-payment. I then asked if there was a way to find out what the balance was, so I could pay it off and unlock the phone. Even though I physically had the phone, could prove that it was not stolen, and could provide the name of the person who had sold it to me, they wouldn't even take such information to look up the account to give me a balance. 
I asked 'Ryan' flat-out to confirm that T-Mobile would rather have an account that was unpaid, and would continue to remain unpaid by a customer who clearly was not interested in paying them, than to have an account paid off, and he said, 'Correct'. He then informed me that it was for 'security purposes' even though, again, I have the physical phone and have this other person's information, as well a signed bill of sale.
At the end of the call I wished Ryan a good day and he said 'I sure will!' Yes, Ryan, I bet you will -- you get paid either way, right? And you and your company definitely don't care whether I become a paying customer. You won't need to worry about that, and I definitely won't be recommending T-Mobile to anyone!

T-Mobile is baaaaaad. I put an order for new phone at end of September and the told me the shipping dat will be between oct,22 to nov, 5. After i wait until oct,22 they pushed the date back to between nov,13 to nov,27.although my brother with me on the family plane did the same order for him on the same day with me and got his new phone after 4 days. It’s a very bad experience with T-Mobile. And I am going to switch the service to something else. By the way this was my second time for me to order the phone the first time the T-Mobile expert did the order wrong and i had to return the phones back to T-Mobile  and do reorder.i have been dealing with this for 2 months. It’s frustrating. Now I canceled the order and I don’t want to deal with T-Mobile again.

After 20 years with sprint/nextel, I switched my service to T-Mobile.  Although the ride with Sprint was bumpy at times, the last several years were good compared to the horrible service coverage from T-mobile.  I live in the Tampa/St Pete area and in the past 6 months I have had more dropped calls, messages of unavailable service and messages of text messages that won't send than I've had with Sprint over the last 10 years combined.  The horrible coverage is not limited to FL.  I have had horrible coverage in the Cleveland, Kansas City, Cincinnati areas.  I will likely switch to another carrier or back to Sprint very soon.  Don't believe the hype on T-mobile.  There gimmicks are not worth the poor service.

I don’t think I’ve ever written a review for anything so I’ll start there. Be careful trading in your devices to get the “free phone” deals. I traded my like new Galaxy S9 in, opened up lines, ported my line in and all those required terms. First bill...not showing the correct plan and charging me for the “free” iPhone XR. A call to customer service resulted in them saying there was an error and it would be fixed. I had to pay the first one. Second bill...showing the correct plan and charging me for the phone. A call to customer service led to the same thing. Want to guess the third and now 4th most recent bill results?

 The latest call yesterday resulted in 45 minutes of questioning me on if I actually traded a phone in because Tmobile systems show no trade in despite having a trade in receipt from the store. They wouldn’t honor the document as proof because it was from a store and not Tmobile customer service (what?), wanting me to contact my old carrier to see if they could give me the IMEI for the old phone I traded in to T-Mobile (which the receipt showed had the serial number but they continued to correct me that I needed specifically the IMEI and, when finding out it was the same thing, continued to talk down to me that I wasn’t using the correct term while using their company receipt), and then after another good wait said “the account was fixed”. I get to pay for the phone again and potentially have credits on my account which I was told would happen 4 times now. 

Again, after all this, be sure you get some kind of confirmation on everything before you hand them you phone and start paying for something “free”.
If I could give zero stars I would, the worst network I ever used.i hate t mobile. I basically pay for the service and dont ever get it.

Horrible customer service. FALSE and MISLEADING INFORMATION.keep me in hold for 2 hours and couldn't solve anything
I was waitting for the manager who hang up on me...they are great in taking your money but horrible in providing service..is all about CAPITALISM

edits.text: Horrible company....they made a mistake on  my account and because of that they send me to collections.... before they give you any information you have to make a payment that you don't even owned....they put in hold multiple times for almost 2 hours.  I have been a customer for years but of course the don't appreciate your business.....big time capitalism.

text: I went into the T-Mobile store in douglasville on Thornton rd.  There were three reps i there, two customers.  There was a lady having a longggg conversation, rep of the reps were engrossed in the conversation, the third one Monique, asked me after a few minutes what I came in for today, put me in the tablet she had, so I stood there over 10 minutes waiting while, I finally got upset and gestured And said  that I am only in here to paymy bill, That is when Monique stopped messing with her tablet, and took my payment , saying to me we like to talk to our customers , and we only have two reps, I understood that they only had two reps, so do your dam  job, and don’t engage in 15 minute conversations, when it does not involve the dam phone service.  I will never go to that dam store again.

Ok how bad is yalls info that u dont know someones correct password... They only set it in 2003 when they got with yall... i dont use tmobile and will never... my sons father does but yall cause so much anxiety cuz yall have bad computers and dont know correct info and its not just one rep its just about every rep u call.... But for some reason they always findout They were wrong and he was right but they fight him and tell him he has wrong password that his password has to be something else well its not its been the same for yrs .... Fix yalls stuff u cant make the same mistake for years and years its just a bad business.... In my opinion why should anyone get with a service they gotta fight with? my mother had A.T.&T. forever and never fought with them like my man has to fight with tmobile...  Get it together Tmobile .... You stress out people that are just close to ur customers... For real.... Your reps hang up and leave him on hold never come back and u wonder why he mad come one hour of being pushed around rep to rep and told he is wrong but it ends up he was giving correct password entire time....

6 solid years with this carrier. Then canceled a line. It was canceled and then they reinstated it. Noticed the extra charges over one year later. It was clear that there is no activity on line that entire time. They refuse to refund me for their mistake. Why on earth would I want a canceled line to randomly reinstate?  Totally shady, or downright fraudulent business practice. Shame on me for not noticing. Shame on them for not refunding their mistake.

I ordered 2 Iphone 11's on Sept. 13. Emobile entered the wrong IMEI number, sent me a shipping label for 1 IPHONE 8 and 1 IPHONE 7. Both iPhone's being sent back was the 8. I called several times (6 to be exact, spoke with guys every time) to make sure that the wrong shipping label (iPhone 7) wouldn't be a problem, I was assured every single time I called about the issue that IT WOULDN'T BE A PROBLEM. Well guess what it was a major problem, I called back one more time and well it was a major problem. The young lady I was speaking with identified the issue right away being the wrong IMEI number. Finally got all of that straight with the iPhones or at least I thought so. I happened to call mobile about an issue and discovered that the insurance on both the new iPhones had been dropped, so I had to go thru the hassle of adding it back on. Then I find out that my corporate discount was never applied to my account the last 2 months, so I had to deal with that issue. SO NOT HAPPY WITH MOBILE RIGHT NOW. THEY HAVE NO CORPORATE NUMBER TO CALL JUST A P.O. BOX.

Our latest experience with T-Mobile Customer Care deserves an A++.  While the TMO towers are getting an upgrade, in our area, the signal strength fluctuates quite a bit.  Contacting TMO Customer Care, we dealt with Judianna. She asked all the right questions, consulted with techs, and got back to us with a solution. Her level of courtesy, patience, and ability to interpret the technology for us, was outstanding.

Worse I mean the worst customer service ever.. paid off my phone and realize a year later they still had it locked. Call customer service to have it unlocked And here come the crap. They saying I can’t unlocked my personal device because I have a payment plant set up for a past due amount. How is that even equatable to having the fon I paid off unlocked is beyond me. Soon as my payment plan kick in I will be leaving them. They truly suck

I just had the worst customer experience with T-Mobile! I no longer have T-Mobile service. It's to high and over priced. So I switched to another phone company. Well I was told by the new phone company and 3 T-Mobile reps that once the number is changed over T-Mobile no longer has access to my account! Well I checked my bank account and T-Mobile tried on several occasions to with drawl money out of my account! Succeed to the point where they overdraft my account!! So I calls customer service to get the issue resolved and they are unable to help me because I don't know the pin to an closed account! So you denied me of help, but can access my account and constantly take money off the card! Crappy  service. NEVER AGAIN WITH T-MOBILE!!!!!!!!

I would give less stars if Google allowed it. Tmobile could not complete a simple phone purchase for our family. My wife has been a client of theirs for  over a decade. We wanted to buy a phone outright. But to complete it you need to do a signature email which by the way is dumb when you are already in the store and they verified your ID via a MN license. Anyway, the email didn't come. So I called on Saturday and was advised to call corporate because the stores can't resend an email. Called corporate. They couldn't resend the email to the email on file without my wife's consent. All we asked was them to resend something that they agreed to on Friday to an email that is on file with them. IE not a different email address which I would expect them to verify that with the wife. We will be switching.
I waited on the phone for 2 hours and 10 minutes and nobody attended to me.
Been with this company over three years and they ain’t loyal to the customer they don’t wanna help you out. Seem like ever month bill gets higher and higher don’t go to this company

I write good reviews and I write bad reviews.TMOBILE IS A LIAR. THEY FRAUD PEOPLE WITH WRONG PROMOTION. They remind me of an EXTORTIONISTS.
Either the staff is trained inadequately or the are stupid. I continue to get the runaround and all they want is money. My bill was 1 day late and the could not unlock my phone until it's paid in full.Customer Service are robots and cannot think on their feet. Tmobile you don't deserve my hard earned cash nor me as a customer. It is apparent that CASH is King in your book. I must bring this to a Judge who can see that you are ripping folks off. No More... You have lost me as a customer forever as well as my  next generation and their Legacy.

Dropping the mic......

The Worst Company EVER.
I do not recommend for anybody.

Too put simply this is a terrible business. You get a phone put it inside a phone case have it break less than 30 days later and they refuse to trade it back because they have such crappy guarantees they last only 14 days like really? You think I am going to figure everything out on my new phone and see if there are any problems by then to report a problem. The second experience was with ANOTHER PHONE that laster no longer than a year replacing it over 5 TIMES!!! Each time having to pay them 5 dollars to replace THEIR FAULTY PHONE! From problems with the GPS not working, screen burning, to physical warping of the phone due to heat they expect me to pay for the new refurbished one. If you are wondering it was the LG G5. Now recently I found out they were charging me for a tablet that they gave to me for FREE as part of a "free promotion" for said crappy phone. Not using it for years cause I already had another tablet found out they were charging me for it. They won't return my money cause the "promotion rebate has closed out". "They," said you needed to go on the website to claim the promotion. Yep sure the only thing they told me was to mail in a 50 dollar rebate for the phone which wasn't even from them but the manufacturer. I think one of their employees set up the promotion as a transaction so he would get more commission off of it than if I was getting it for free. I'm done with these people I'm going to find another carrier that won't be treating me like absolute garbage. If they ever want me to recommend them they might have to dig up my corpse and puppeteer my body for that to happen!

published review 10/11/19
In summary, here are the issues we encountered with T-mobile:
- Coverage map is false advertising and flat out inaccurate (which after calling T-mobile they confirmed an area they claimed to have service in fact does not) - Customer service is brutal as you get bounced around reps that you can’t understand, have no authority, and never get back to you like they say they will
- You signup for the monthly auto payment and it routinely just forgets the info and doesnt refill causing you to lose service.
In all the services we use as a couple, this has been by far the worst choice we’ve made. It has nothing to do with the money. The amount of frustration that been caused by T-mobile has put us on a mission to share our story. I cannot stress enough how terrible this company is. If anyone wants to learn more or verify our situation, happy to discuss further or share more details.
Do yourself a massive favor, and DO NOT SIGN UP FOR T MOBILE

I just cancelled my T-Mobile coverage this month. They charged me $235 for the month of September because I’m transferring to ATT. I was told I’m being charged $470 esvp fees for back fees I owe and I paid out $227 for what was left on my 2 phones these people cost me a lot of headaches & money my termination better had gone thru, I will never return to this company or recommend them to friends. T-Mobile is the worst...buyer beware!!!

I wish I had seen these reviews prior to trying T-Mobile. Their sales people are very good and very personable. I liked them very much and they are the reason I tried them in the first place. It didn't take long to realize that they promised many things, but delivered very few. In the end, I couldn't even get the courtesy of a phone call just to save an eight line business account. Unfortunately, this looks like it is par for the course when it comes to T-Mobile. Getting new customers is not their problem...keeping them is apparently a real challenge. It's a real shame for a company with outwardly motivated and knowledgeable employees to have an anemic foundation of support and follow-through.

Customer service person lied to get the sale, then assured me that I wouldn’t lose my place in line for a back ordered phone when using my fiancé’s card to originally place the order as I didn’t have my card with me at the time. Stated they would contact me if any issue came up with billing for my new phone. As soon as they emailed me a confirmation that the card on file needed to be updated I called and gave them a new payment option to find out I now lost my place in line and will need to wait an entire month because of the back order. Had I had known this information I would have waited to place the order with my own card at the time. I’ve been a customer with Tmobile for 15 years and due to this I will be looking to cancel switch to Verizon. They also never informed me that the Apple Watch I ordered prior to my phone was not compatible with my current phone. Needless to say I have a watch that will not work and am last on the list for my iPhone. Will be returning all products recently purchased including an iPad on my account.

We got a jump on demand phone and I broke the glass. I took it in and was told that I could either pay $200 and get it replaced or I could just get a new phone and keep paying on this phone for about 9 more months and be done then. I decided on the second option because it was far less expensive. Turns out it was for much longer and there is a fee at the end as well. It ended up being far more money to finish out the phone than it was to just pay the money and move on from it. I called T-Mobile on multiple occasions and they did help out a bit but didn't seem the least bit concerned that their employees were telling me lies.

Worst buying experience I've ever had. Both the salesman and manager lied to us about our monthly bill. We were told our bill would be $200 a month with the 2 free tablets they had on promotion. We were told this MANY MANY times throughout the whole experience. 
Our bill did not reflect that when we got it in. We called, the first lady we spoke to said she would call us back, she never did. 
The second person we spoke to said that the store lied to us. He stated he would take care of it and got out bill down to $200.37/mo. from here on out. Perfect, we thought.
Fast forward a month and our bill went back up. So we call. The supervisor states that the notes in the system say that the employee we spoke to did a 1 time courtesy discount for us. If we would have known that, we would have cancelled the service then and there. She states there is nothing she can do for us. 
We were lied to by the South Mall store, and we were lied to by customer service.
The best part is, the manager stated, even if they go to the recording of the phone call, and the guy did say our bill would be $200/mo. that their would still be nothing that they can do. Not to mention it's past the point we can return everything. So our options are, pay for the service at a higher price than we were quoted on multiple occasions. Or, cancel the service but pay out of pocket for all the devices. 
They lied, and even if they realize that they lied, they still won't fix it. Stay far far away from this company and store. Trap artists.

My experience with T-Mobile was relatively decent until I decided to cancel service.  I will say that I believe their coverage maps are deceptive.  T-Mobile was by far the poorest coverage I've ever experienced, and outside of a major city you can pretty much count on zero talk/data coverage with rare exceptions.  Early in June I cancelled all of my service.  However, the agent I spoke with failed to cancel one of the lines and I received another bill the next month for the full family data plan and one line.  I called to dispute the bill and the agent assured me that I would be credited and not to worry about it.  A month later, I got billed for another month!  It has now been almost 4 months and approximately 10 calls to customer service since I cancelled.  Now I'm getting late payment fees on their errors.  I could not possibly give T-Mobile a lower recommendation.  The service is terrible and the employees are incompetent.

If there was a 0 star option I would give that.  Coverage in the Phoenix area is horrible.  Our house had zero bars even though we were told we'd have great coverage based on our address.  We left after 6 months and now cannot get customer services to help with ANYTHING.  Online customer service says to go to the store.  Store reps say to call the support #.  It is quite a joke and I cannot believe T Mobile can get away with this awful service and product when we have so many other options.

I recommend not to trust T-Mobile offers. They cheat customers on giving attracting offers. I purchased free phone with T-Mobile offer (100$ refill), the phone unlock does not work even after requirements are met.

Poor Tech & Poor Service: Many dropped calls (30%+) while in a major metropolitan area. Spent nearly an hour trying to find the correct number to call (their website was not functioning), then over 27 minutes on hold listening to rap music. I must have heard over 25 times the rap stop (thankfully) followed by a robot telling me the great thing about T-Mobile is that I won't have to speak with a robot. Then back to rap. Equally annoying and ironic! Finally reached someone who had a great attitude but alas very little command of the English language. I literally understood less than half of what she said. But to her credit she must have understood me because she reset some tower in my area and that apparently worked. But now I'm exhausted. Just about any carrier has got to be better than T-Mobile. To be fair, the support person was really trying her best. I fault T-Mobile for putting her on the phone in the first place and for the 27 minute hold time. Hence the 1 star on service.

customer service at T-mobile when I just talked with them were FUKKKING GREAT. So nice. so helpful. I've had a problem with the service a cpl times now. the person I talked with, rachel, was super super nice and scheduled a follow up call with me next week just to make sure that my issue has been resolved. Wow.

The worst cell phone company customer service ever



T-Mobile used to have excellent customer service. Now they have the worst, of any company I have ever experienced. No one individual in any department has any idea what the procedures are for doing anything in their department. Then if you have to be transferred between departments, they actually start to contradict each other.



I have been on the phone with T-Mobile every day for the past 10 days, for an average of about 2 hours.



Someone hacked into my phone and my computer. So I had to call T-Mobile to change the password for my online account. The person I spoke to said they couldn't change the password. What they could do instead was disable my online account, so that no one - including me - would be able to access it. I said great. So they went ahead and disabled the online account.

Then it turned out that the same person had also hacked my tablet. So I had to buy a tablet, and called T-Mobile again for that reason. The person I talked to said they were going to charge me $15 or something like that because I was calling on the phone! instead of using my online account. I said, "How can you charge me, when my online account is disabled for safety purposes?" The person said, "It is not disabled for security purposes. There is no such thing. We have to charge you the $15." I said, "It's not disabled for security purposes! So how can I make sure the hacker doesn't have access to it? Can you change the password over the phone for me?" He said, "Yes, that is what we have to do. But I don't know how to do it." And he transferred me back to the original department, the department that told me that changing passwords over the phone WAS NOT POSSIBLE.

The person I spoke to this time confirmed what the original person had said. "We cannot change your password over the phone. What we can do instead is disable your online account. And in fact it has already been disabled." I said, "So why will I be charged $15 for calling you on the phone to place an order, instead of going online? When IT WAS YOUR IDEA to prohibit me from going online." They transferred me again.

The next person I talked to agreed that I should not be charged the $15, and "waived" the charge. Note that this entire process took about 3 hours.



In that initial conversation when they disabled my online account, I asked them to change my email address, because the hacker had also hacked my email accounts - obviously.

Then this guy - the hacker - hacked my computer AGAIN. I was forced to call T-Mobile and ask them to change the email address again. This time they said, "We are never allowed to change your email address for you. You have to do it yourself. Do you want us to take the high-security lock off your account?" 

???

I said, "If that's true, why did the people I talk to before say the exact opposite, AND change the email address for me?" The person said, "Who said that / did that?" Like I'm supposed to remember right? 

I asked to speak to a manager. This person said "Oh Angela, we completely understand. We will look into changing the email address for you over the phone." They put me on hold for 25 minutes. Then they came back on and said, "Angela, we have taken the security lock off your account, so you can go and change the email address yourself." 

??? 

I said, "Someone is continually hacking me. I don't want the security lock to be taken off my account. As I mentioned to you earlier in this conversation, and as I mentioned to the person who transferred me to you, I'm trying to get you to change the email address for me, like you did before." 

They said, "Oh ok, sorry. We'll look into that." Which is exactly the same thing they had said before. 

They put me on hold for 40 minutes this time. 

Then they came back saying they couldn't change the email address after all.

I hung up and called back. The person I talked to this time said they had no idea what was wrong with the previous two people, as changing an email address is very easy. It's 5 clicks of a mouse. Then they changed my email address.



At another point, I was told by a computer technician that I would have to change my phone number, because the same thing (hacking) had happened to them, and the police had advised them to change their number.

I called T-Mobile to change the number. I was on hold for about 30 minutes before getting anyone. The person I talked to was having a very hard time understanding ANYTHING I said - including my name or words like "How are you?" It was an American, not someone in another country, and the phone connection was perfectly fine - at least on my end (I wasn't having trouble hearing her). While I was giving her my name, she did sound like she was half-asleep, like she could care less about her job and wanted to get off the phone. Now, when you call in and do business with them over the phone, they ask for the pin or passcode on your account. When I gave this woman - who was half-asleep - the passcode, she said that it was wrong (it wasn't). I told her she must have entered it wrong, and wanted to give it to her again. She said that that wasn't an option, that I would have to call back. So I had to hang up, call back, wait on hold for another 20 minutes, and then gave the SAME passcode, and this time got into the account.



These are only 3 examples of the conversations I have had with them this week. There are more. I don't have the time or energy to get into it. I don't have time for these conversations, so have had to cancel my account with them, effective one month from now.

This whole experience (getting hacked) has been a nightmare, and Bank of America, Venmo, and ESPECIALLY T-MOBILE have compounded that nightmare several times over. T-Mobile has become one of the worst companies I have ever, ever dealt with. DO NOT EVER USE T-MOBILE.







UPDATE: I don't know what the other companies are like, so today, I went around to various cell phone stores (including those of other providers) asking questions. I went to Boost Mobile and bought a phone case that wasn't available at T-Mobile. Then I popped into a couple of T-Mobile stores as well, to check out their phones and finalize my decision. 

One of the stores I went to was the one on 180th & Broadway: 4260 Broadway. While checking out the phones, I checked my email and changed some info on one of them. One of the people who worked there, named Moises, informed me that I wasn't allowed to do that. I said, "Oh ok," and quickly finished writing down the info from my email account, signed out, and cleared the phone's history. Then for the next 5 minutes I walked around, looking at other phones, and among other things I continued to browse the Internet on them. This T-Mobile CUSTOMER SERVICE representative named Moises, then suddenly started YELLING AT ME FROM ACROSS THE STORE, that "Hey miss! You aren't allowed to browse the web on those phones!" Then he actually came over, stalked in front of me, and clicked on the phone so that I couldn't access the Internet. He then proceeded to turn around and start exclaiming very loudly to the other customer service representatives that I had been doing personal things on the phone, when I wasn't allowed to.

In fact, originally I had not known that I was not allowed to. When Moises told me that I wasn't, I finished writing a note down, then I stopped. Then I went around and looked at the other phones, and proceeded to BROWSE THE WEB on them. Nobody, including this individual completely lacking in class & the minimum of manners, had said that you are not allowed to browse the web - and in fact, if you're not, why is the Internet accessible from so many of these phones? Aren't you supposed to be able to look at that, as well as other features on the phone? Nevertheless, had he told me that I was not allowed to do that, I would not have. Instead, he had said nothing about it, and then he started yelling from across the store, talking very loudly about it to the other agents, and then practically pushed me out of the way so I wouldn't access the Internet on the phone. Instead of saying, politely, and informatively, "Miss, I didn't only mean that you are not allowed to check your email on the phone. What I meant was that you are not allowed to go online at all." 

Maybe Moises didn't say that because it wasn't true. Instead, he just wanted an excuse to be rude? Because he likes to be? 

T-Mobile has horrendous customer service. When I mentioned this, MOISES STARTED LAUGHING, and so did the other agents.

Visit this T-Mobile store if you want your horrendous phone customer service experience to be replicated in real life. And ask for Moises.

I have been using Tmobile for about 6 years. This review has to do with mistakes in accounting and assurances that Black Friday iPhone X special was 100% covered with a trade in of iPhone 6. I was told I would have a credit every month for 36 months. I bought a second phone and a few months later, I went to a Tmobile outlet and paid off the second phone( I thought). Turns out that the rep put the money on the free first phone and continued to charge me for the second phone. It took hours to get to a supervisor who refunded the money I paid and then called me so that I could pay off the second phone. Then the surprise came. I was told I would be paying $3 a month on the first phone. I argued that the phone was represented as free. John, the supervisor just did not understand. Buyer beware. Get it in writing. Do not believe what sales people tell you. Have them write it down and get their name.

Would give less than 0 stars if possible. Went into t mobile about a year ago to get out of verizon, the gentleman in the store was very adamant about the "get out of the red program" that they were running, seemed like a good deal to have pur phones paid off for us via the offer. After paying off my verizon devices and sending them in the final bill statement that was requested for reimbursement we were told that the bill we sent was not itemized (it was) and therefore not eligible for reimbursement.  1100$ down the drain in the first month! What a deal! Stay with me as this gets better. After about a year of enduring the worst coverage I've ever had with a carrier we decided to go back to Verizon.  After having our phones ported over and being told that our account had been closed with t mobile, I kept getting $70 charges to my credit card. Turns out just because I had cancelled my phone lines with them and been told that my account was closed, there was still a tablet associated with a phone number/ account that I had not had access to. Long story short they were unwilling to reimburse me for those charges and wouldn't even prorate the service for the tablet on cancellation day. I would avoid this company like the plague as this is $1300 in total that has been a complete wash over the course of a year.  Customer service was a joke and the company as a whole seems to be extremely shady in practice.

The worst part is they seem to want to do nothing about this. Pull your calls I know you record! Nope now I am out the money and he has to come up with it on a payment plan. Tmobile will scam you! Beware!

TMobile used to have superior service, but not for the last year and a half. The last 4 or 5 trips to the retail store I've left disappointed or angry. It seems lately TMobile has deals that they offer that don't exsist when you get to the store or the stipulations are so tough that you can't or don't want to do it. It covers the insurance for the phone and is supposed to give you the ability to trade phones a number of times a year if you want the nee model. The last year they look for reasons for me not to be able to Jump. Now they require you to  have half to 3 quarters down for the new phone and this wasn't required to jump. I knew merging with Spring was going to be a mistake but Tmobile's customer service is horrific and dealing with Corporate is just as bad when you call 611. They don't give a damn if they resolve anything making twice as angry. I guess a 5 year customer doesn't mean anything because I am looking for other service as soon as possible and tell everyone I know that are Tmobile customers also why....

My name is Ethan Brown i never write reviews but i had to. I needed a new phone so Today I bought a T-mobile  phone with a case for $300 at 10am. It was ths worst decision I couldve made. Roughly 5 min after purchasing the phone from the T-mobile store I dropped the phone, from about 5 feet high after stepping off my bike, with the case on it. The phone completely exploded on impact with the ground. It caught fire melting my otterbox case and earbuds. So I immediately return to the store to show them. They inform me that I needed to contact the T-mobile insurance on the phone in order to get a replacement phone the next day. So I did that at about 10:30am. The cut off to get the replacement phone the next day is 8pm. So I called the insurance and went through all kinds of hell. I was sent from one support staff to the other i talked to over 10 T-mobile support members. All of them saying they were going to get it done and that itll work out. I went through that circle for over 7 hours calling back and forth, getting no where having to retell the same story over and over and giving my information with every new support member. Each one of them guaranteeing that they would be the last person I would have to talk to. It took them so long that I missed the cut off time, thats 8 hours that I called before the cut off time and in those 8 hours I got absolutly no where they had no idea agat they were doing. I work for favor so I have to have a phone in order to work. I didnt get to work today because of this situation and because its Thursday today and they couldnt get it done in the cut off time i will have to wait until monday before i get my new phone that means at least 4 days with no phone for me. That means I wont get to work or make money for the next 4 days. And im 21 in austin with no family support. Im not someone in a position to miss 4 days of work. Thanks to T-mobile I will be at a new all time low with all my money that i have in the phone i jjst bought and know i cant work to make more.  Thanks for reading. 

Ethan Brown
